generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use existing, visible AADL features in AGREE expressions; avoid introducing new architecture.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) are correct and resolve all references.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Output assignments should use the `assign` keyword.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL components or connections as part of a repair.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
Do not attempt to fix type errors by modifying the AADL model.
